task: What events do I have October 22 2023 in Simple Calendar Pro? Answer with the titles only. If there are multiple titles, format your answer as a comma separated list.

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the Android home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and another Gmail shortcut. The target app, "Simple Calendar Pro", is not visible on this main home screen page.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open Simple Calendar Pro app → | Step 2: Navigate to Sunday's date (Oct 20) | Step 3: Extract event titles for that day | Step 4: Format and output the titles as a comma-separated list

[Decision]
Since I am on the home screen and the target app "Simple Calendar Pro" is not among the visible shortcuts, I need to launch it. I will use the system's open app function to start "Simple Calendar Pro" directly to proceed with checking the schedule.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The screen displays the Simple Calendar Pro app in a monthly view for October. Today's date is October 15th (highlighted with a circle). The upcoming Sunday is October 20th, which has an event titled "Catch up" listed on it.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Simple Calendar Pro app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Sunday's date (Oct 20) → | Step 3: Extract event titles for that day | Step 4: Format and output the titles as a comma-separated list

[Decision]
According to the plan, I need to check the events for Sunday, October 20th. I can see the cell for the 20th on the calendar grid, but it has only one event partially visible ("Catch up"). To see all events for that day clearly and ensure there are no others, I should tap on the cell for October 20th to open the daily view.

[Memory]
Target date: Sunday, October 20th. Current partial observation: "Catch up" is scheduled for Oct 20. Need to check if there are more events.

[Observation]
The screen displays the daily schedule view for "October 20 (Fri)". There is one event listed: "Catch up on Budget Planning" from 06:10 to 06:25. The date shown at the top is Friday, October 20th. Navigation arrows are present next to the date to go to the previous or next day.

[Reflection]
The previous plan assumed October 20th was Sunday based on the monthly view, but the daily view explicitly shows "October 20 (Fri)". The target date "Sunday" is actually October 20th + 2 days = October 22nd (though wait, let's check the navigation arrows). The current date is Friday, Oct 20. The goal is Sunday, so I need to navigate forward.

[Replan]
Step 1: Open Simple Calendar Pro app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Sunday's date (Oct 22) → | Step 3: Extract event titles for Sunday | Step 4: Format and output the titles as a comma-separated list

[Decision]
Since the current view is for Friday, October 20th, and the target is Sunday, I need to advance the date. I will tap the right arrow next to the date header to move to the next day, which will be Saturday, October 21st, and then I'll need to go to Sunday (Oct 22). I'll tap the next day button.

[Memory]
Target date: Sunday. Current view is Friday, Oct 20th. "Catch up on Budget Planning" is scheduled for Friday. Need to reach Sunday (Oct 22).

[Observation]
The screen shows the daily schedule view for "October 21 (Sat)". There are three events listed: "Appointment for Project X" (02:21 - 02:36), "Catch up on Annual Report" (19:27 - 20:27), and "Review session for Annual Report" (19:56 - 20:26), and "Workshop on Campaign" (21:00 - 21:45). The date is Saturday.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Simple Calendar Pro app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Sunday's date (Oct 22) → | Step 3: Extract event titles for Sunday | Step 4: Format and output the titles as a comma-separated list

[Decision]
The current view is for Saturday, October 21st. The target is Sunday (October 22nd). To reach Sunday, I need to advance the date one more time. I will tap the right arrow next to the date header again to move to the next day.

[Memory]
Target date: Sunday (Oct 22). Current view is Saturday, Oct 21st. Events for Saturday are "Appointment for Project X", "Catch up on Annual Report", "Review session for Annual Report", and "Workshop on Campaign". Need to reach Sunday next.

[Observation]
The screen displays the daily schedule view for "October 22 (Sun)". There is only one event listed: "Board meeting" scheduled from 10:30 to 12:00.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Simple Calendar Pro app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to Sunday's date (Oct 22) ✓ | Step 3: Extract event titles for Sunday ✓ | Step 4: Format and output the titles as a comma-separated list →

[Decision]
The current view is on Sunday, October 22nd. I can see the event title "Board meeting". There are no other events scheduled for this day. According to the task instructions, I need to answer with the titles only. Since there is only one event, I will provide the final answer directly to the user by speaking the title "Board meeting".

[Memory]
Target date reached: Sunday, Oct 22. Event found: "Board meeting". No other events for this day. Task is ready to be completed.
